steps.title: Identify what supports the conclusion
steps.content:
The conclusion is that neither V. fischeri nor B. fragilis likely played a significant role in the development of multicellularity.

To support this, the data should show that S. rosetta does not respond to either bacterium by forming colonies (the colony-forming behavior linked to multicellularity).

steps.title: Match the finding
steps.content:
Choice D states that S. rosetta did not form colonies with either bacterium — exactly what supports the conclusion.

The other choices show colony formation with at least one bacterium, which would contradict the conclusion.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
